words.meaning_en: To make something bad or unsatisfactory better
words.example_sentence: The government took steps to ameliorate the living conditions of slum dwellers.

one_word_sub.question: To make something better or improve a bad situation

words.sentence: New policies were introduced to ______ the suffering of flood victims.
words.example_sentence_hi: सरकार ने झुग्गी वासियों की स्थिति सुधारने के लिए कदम उठाए।

words.meaning_en: A harsh, discordant mixture of sounds
words.example_sentence: The cacophony of traffic horns made it impossible to think.

one_word_sub.question: A harsh and unpleasant mixture of loud sounds

words.sentence: The street market was filled with the ______ of vendors calling out loudly.
words.example_sentence_hi: यातायात के हॉर्न की कर्कश ध्वनि ने सोचना असंभव बना दिया।

words.meaning_en: A person or thing that announces or signals the approach of another
words.example_sentence: Dark clouds are often a harbinger of rain.

one_word_sub.question: Something that indicates the approach of a future event

words.sentence: The cuckoo's call is considered a ______ of spring.
words.example_sentence_hi: काले बादल अक्सर बारिश के अग्रदूत होते हैं।

words.meaning_en: Using very few words; brief and concise
words.example_sentence: His laconic reply — just 'No' — surprised everyone.

one_word_sub.question: A person who uses very few words to express themselves

words.sentence: The general was known for his ______ orders that left no room for confusion.
words.example_sentence_hi: उनका संक्षिप्त उत्तर — केवल 'नहीं' — ने सभी को चौंका दिया।

words.meaning_en: Wicked, criminal, or extremely immoral
words.example_sentence: The nefarious scheme was uncovered by the investigators.

one_word_sub.question: An extremely wicked or criminal act or plan

words.sentence: The gang was involved in ______ activities that harmed the community.
words.example_sentence_hi: जाँचकर्ताओं ने उस दुष्टतापूर्ण योजना को उजागर किया।

words.meaning_en: Having an obstinately uncooperative attitude
words.example_sentence: The recalcitrant employee refused to follow the new policy.

one_word_sub.question: A person who stubbornly refuses to obey rules or authority

words.sentence: The ______ student was sent to the principal's office for the third time.
words.example_sentence_hi: अड़ियल कर्मचारी ने नई नीति का पालन करने से इनकार कर दिया।

words.meaning_en: Deliberate and unprovoked; showing reckless disregard
words.example_sentence: The wanton destruction of public property is a criminal offence.

one_word_sub.question: An act of deliberate cruelty or destruction without reason

words.sentence: The ______ cruelty shown to animals shocked the entire neighbourhood.
words.example_sentence_hi: सार्वजनिक संपत्ति का जानबूझकर नुकसान एक आपराधिक अपराध है।

words.meaning_en: To leave hurriedly and secretly to escape from legal consequences
words.example_sentence: The cashier absconded with the company's funds.

one_word_sub.question: To leave a place secretly to escape from the law

words.sentence: The suspect managed to ______ before the police arrived.
words.example_sentence_hi: कैशियर कंपनी का पैसा लेकर फरार हो गया।

words.meaning_en: Something that deviates from what is standard or expected
words.example_sentence: The scientist found an anomaly in the data that suggested an error.

one_word_sub.question: Something that deviates from the normal or expected rule

words.sentence: The sudden drop in temperature was an ______ for the month of June.
words.example_sentence_hi: वैज्ञानिक को डेटा में एक अपवाद मिला जो त्रुटि का संकेत देता था।

words.meaning_en: Extreme greed for wealth or material gain
words.example_sentence: His avarice led him to betray his closest friends.

one_word_sub.question: Excessive greed for wealth or material possessions

words.sentence: His ______ blinded him to the suffering he caused.
words.example_sentence_hi: उसके लोभ ने उसे अपने निकटतम मित्रों को धोखा देने पर मजबूर किया।

words.meaning_en: To persuade someone to do something by flattery or gentle argument
words.example_sentence: He tried to cajole his teacher into extending the deadline.

one_word_sub.question: To persuade someone through flattery or gentle pleading

words.sentence: She managed to ______ the committee into approving her proposal.
words.example_sentence_hi: उसने अपने शिक्षक को समयसीमा बढ़ाने के लिए मनाने की कोशिश की।

words.meaning_en: To persuade someone using force, threats, or pressure
words.example_sentence: The suspect claimed he was coerced into signing the confession.

one_word_sub.question: To force someone to do something against their will

words.sentence: Workers should never be ______ into accepting unfair terms.
words.example_sentence_hi: आरोपी ने दावा किया कि उसे बयान पर हस्ताक्षर करने के लिए मजबूर किया गया।

words.hindi: भोला / आसानी से विश्वास करने वाला
words.meaning_en: Having too much readiness to believe; gullible
words.example_sentence: A credulous person is easily deceived by false advertisements.

one_word_sub.question: A person who is too ready to believe things without evidence

words.sentence: Con artists often target ______ elderly people.
words.example_sentence_hi: एक भोला व्यक्ति झूठे विज्ञापनों से आसानी से धोखा खाता है।

words.meaning_en: A scarcity or lack of something
words.example_sentence: There is a dearth of trained doctors in rural areas.

one_word_sub.question: A condition of scarcity or insufficient supply

words.sentence: The report highlighted a ______ of investment in public health.
words.example_sentence_hi: ग्रामीण क्षेत्रों में प्रशिक्षित डॉक्टरों की कमी है।

words.meaning_en: A lack of harmony; a clash between opposing ideas or sounds
words.example_sentence: There was a cognitive dissonance between his words and actions.

one_word_sub.question: A tension arising from two opposing or inconsistent ideas existing simultaneously

words.sentence: The ______ between government policy and ground reality was striking.
words.example_sentence_hi: उसके शब्दों और कार्यों के बीच एक संज्ञानात्मक विसंगति थी।

words.meaning_en: To cause someone to feel drained of energy or vitality
words.example_sentence: The long march enervated the soldiers.

one_word_sub.question: To weaken or drain the energy and strength of a person

words.sentence: The tropical heat began to ______ the rescue workers.
words.example_sentence_hi: लंबे मार्च ने सैनिकों को कमज़ोर कर दिया।

words.meaning_en: To make a problem, situation, or bad feeling worse
words.example_sentence: The drought exacerbated the already severe food crisis.

one_word_sub.question: To make an already bad problem or situation worse

words.sentence: Delay in relief ______ the suffering of the flood victims.
words.example_sentence_hi: सूखे ने पहले से गंभीर खाद्य संकट को और बिगाड़ दिया।

words.meaning_en: To remove completely; to wipe out or erase from a record
words.example_sentence: He petitioned the court to expunge the crime from his record.

one_word_sub.question: To completely remove or erase something from a record or memory

words.sentence: The convicted man's minor offence was ______ from the record after twenty years.
words.example_sentence_hi: उसने अपने रिकॉर्ड से अपराध को मिटाने के लिए अदालत में याचिका दायर की।

words.meaning_en: Excessively talkative, especially on trivial matters
words.example_sentence: The garrulous witness kept the court waiting with his long-winded answers.

one_word_sub.question: A person who talks excessively, especially about trivial things

words.sentence: The ______ neighbour shared every detail of her day with anyone who would listen.
words.example_sentence_hi: वाचाल गवाह ने अपने लंबे जवाबों से अदालत को इंतजार कराया।

words.meaning_en: Not able to be corrected, improved, or reformed
words.example_sentence: The judge called him an incorrigible offender and gave a harsh sentence.

one_word_sub.question: A person who repeatedly behaves badly and cannot be reformed

words.sentence: Despite many warnings, he remained an ______ troublemaker.
words.example_sentence_hi: न्यायाधीश ने उसे असुधार्य अपराधी कहकर कठोर सज़ा सुनाई।

words.meaning_en: Tending to talk a great deal; very talkative
words.example_sentence: The loquacious presenter never let anyone else speak.

one_word_sub.question: A person who talks excessively and at great length

words.sentence: The ______ politician dominated the entire press conference.
words.example_sentence_hi: बातूनी प्रस्तुतकर्ता ने कभी किसी को बोलने नहीं दिया।

words.meaning_en: Excessively submissive or eager to please in a servile way
words.example_sentence: His obsequious flattery was transparent to everyone in the office.

one_word_sub.question: A person who is excessively obedient and eager to please superiors

words.sentence: The ______ assistant agreed with everything the manager said.
words.example_sentence_hi: उनकी अत्यधिक चापलूसी दफ्तर में सभी को दिखती थी।

words.meaning_en: A person who is rejected or excluded by society or a group
words.example_sentence: After the scandal, he became a social pariah.

one_word_sub.question: A person who is universally rejected or excluded from a social group

words.sentence: The country became an international ______ after the invasion.
words.example_sentence_hi: घोटाले के बाद, वह सामाजिक रूप से बहिष्कृत हो गया।

words.meaning_en: Using or containing too many words; long and tedious
words.example_sentence: The prolix report could have been summarised in two pages.

one_word_sub.question: A piece of writing or speech that is unnecessarily long and wordy

words.sentence: The professor's ______ lecture ran for three hours without a break.
words.example_sentence_hi: लंबी-चौड़ी रिपोर्ट को दो पृष्ठों में संक्षिप्त किया जा सकता था।

words.meaning_en: Reserved or uncommunicative in speech; saying little
words.example_sentence: The taciturn minister gave only a brief statement.

one_word_sub.question: A person who habitually says little and is uncommunicative

words.example_sentence_hi: अल्पभाषी मंत्री ने केवल एक संक्षिप्त बयान दिया।

words.meaning_en: An award or privilege granted as a special honour
words.example_sentence: The scientist received the highest accolade for her groundbreaking research.

one_word_sub.question: An expression of praise or special recognition given to someone

words.sentence: Winning the national championship was the greatest ______ of her athletic career.
words.example_sentence_hi: वैज्ञानिक को उनके अभूतपूर्व शोध के लिए सर्वोच्च प्रशंसा मिली।

words.meaning_en: Warn or reprimand someone firmly
words.example_sentence: The judge admonished the lawyer for his unprofessional conduct.

one_word_sub.question: To warn or scold someone firmly

words.sentence: The teacher ______ the student for repeatedly disrupting the class.
words.example_sentence_hi: न्यायाधीश ने वकील को उसके अव्यावसायिक आचरण के लिए चेतावनी दी।

words.example_sentence: The government tried to appease the protesters by announcing relief measures.

one_word_sub.question: To calm or pacify someone by giving in to their demands

words.sentence: The manager tried to ______ the unhappy customers with a refund.
words.example_sentence_hi: सरकार ने राहत उपायों की घोषणा करके प्रदर्शनकारियों को शांत करने की कोशिश की।

words.meaning_en: Involving great effort or difficulty
words.example_sentence: Climbing Everest is an arduous task that requires months of preparation.

one_word_sub.question: A task that requires great effort and is extremely difficult

words.sentence: The ______ journey through the mountains tested the limits of the expedition team.
words.example_sentence_hi: एवरेस्ट पर चढ़ना एक कठिन कार्य है जिसके लिए महीनों की तैयारी चाहिए।

words.meaning_en: Well-meaning and kindly; charitable
words.example_sentence: The benevolent donor funded scholarships for hundreds of underprivileged students.

one_word_sub.question: A person who is kind and wishes good to others

words.sentence: The ______ king was loved by all for his care and generosity.
words.example_sentence_hi: परोपकारी दानकर्ता ने सैकड़ों वंचित छात्रों के लिए छात्रवृत्ति दी।

words.meaning_en: Done openly and unashamedly; conspicuously obvious

one_word_sub.question: Something done openly without any attempt to hide it

words.sentence: The politician's ______ disregard for the law shocked the public.
words.example_sentence_hi: यह एक स्पष्ट झूठ था जिसे हर कोई देख सकता था।

words.meaning_en: Wary and unwilling to take risks; cautious

one_word_sub.question: A person who is cautious and considers all consequences before acting

one_word_sub.question: An activity carried out secretly, especially for illegal purposes

words.example_sentence: Several witnesses came forward to corroborate her account of events.

one_word_sub.question: To confirm or support an argument or statement with evidence

words.sentence: The scientific data ______ the hypothesis proposed by the research team.

words.meaning_en: Distrustful of human sincerity or integrity; believing people are motivated by self-interest

words.meaning_en: Showing fearlessness and determination
words.example_sentence: The dauntless soldier continued fighting despite being outnumbered.

one_word_sub.question: A person who shows courage without fear

words.sentence: Her ______ spirit inspired the entire team to push through adversity.
words.example_sentence_hi: निडर सैनिक संख्या में कम होने के बावजूद लड़ता रहा।

words.meaning_en: Perceive or recognize something
words.example_sentence: It takes wisdom to discern truth from propaganda.

one_word_sub.question: To perceive or recognize something that is not obvious

words.sentence: A good judge can ______ between genuine distress and false claims.
words.example_sentence_hi: सच्चाई को प्रचार से पहचानने के लिए बुद्धि की आवश्यकता होती है।

words.meaning_en: Famous and respected within a particular sphere

one_word_sub.question: A person who is famous and respected in their field

words.sentence: The award was presented by an ______ scientist who had contributed to space research.
words.example_sentence_hi: जाँच आयोग का नेतृत्व करने के लिए एक प्रतिष्ठित न्यायविद नियुक्त किया गया।

words.meaning_en: Tending to avoid commitment or direct answers
words.example_sentence: The witness gave evasive answers that frustrated the prosecutor.

one_word_sub.question: A person who avoids giving direct and clear answers

words.sentence: The minister's ______ response to the journalist's question raised more suspicions.
words.example_sentence_hi: गवाह के टालमटोल भरे जवाबों ने अभियोजक को निराश किया।

words.meaning_en: Having or displaying a passionate intensity
words.example_sentence: She was a fervent supporter of environmental conservation.

one_word_sub.question: A person who is extremely passionate and enthusiastic about something

words.sentence: The ______ speech of the activist inspired hundreds to join the movement.
words.example_sentence_hi: वह पर्यावरण संरक्षण की उत्साही समर्थक थी।

words.meaning_en: Fond of company; sociable
words.example_sentence: The gregarious student quickly made friends in the new school.

one_word_sub.question: A person who enjoys the company of others and is very sociable

words.sentence: Her ______ personality made her a natural choice for the team leader role.
words.example_sentence_hi: मिलनसार छात्र ने नए स्कूल में जल्दी दोस्त बना लिए।

words.meaning_en: A person who claims to have virtues they do not actually possess
words.example_sentence: He was exposed as a hypocrite who preached honesty but cheated on his taxes.

one_word_sub.question: A person who pretends to be virtuous but acts against those virtues

words.sentence: The self-proclaimed reformer was branded a ______ when his corrupt dealings were exposed.
words.example_sentence_hi: वह एक पाखंडी के रूप में उजागर हुआ जो ईमानदारी का उपदेश देता था लेकिन करों में धोखा देता था।

words.example_sentence: An indolent student will struggle to keep up with the competitive exam syllabus.

one_word_sub.question: A person who is habitually lazy and avoids work

words.example_sentence_hi: एक आलसी छात्र प्रतियोगी परीक्षा के पाठ्यक्रम के साथ तालमेल बनाए रखने में संघर्ष करेगा।

words.meaning_en: Clever, original, and inventive
words.example_sentence: The engineer came up with an ingenious solution to reduce water wastage.

one_word_sub.question: A person who is very clever and creative in finding solutions

words.sentence: The startup developed an ______ app that solved a long-standing civic problem.
words.example_sentence_hi: इंजीनियर ने पानी की बर्बादी कम करने के लिए एक चतुर समाधान निकाला।

words.meaning_en: Fearless and adventurous
words.example_sentence: The intrepid journalist traveled to war zones to report the truth.

one_word_sub.question: A person who is fearless and willing to face danger

words.sentence: The ______ explorer mapped uncharted territories in the Amazon jungle.
words.example_sentence_hi: निडर पत्रकार सच्चाई की रिपोर्ट करने के लिए युद्ध क्षेत्रों में गया।

words.meaning_en: Displaying or having a disinclination for physical exertion or effort
words.example_sentence: He lay in a languid pose in the summer afternoon heat.

one_word_sub.question: A person or manner showing a lack of energy or enthusiasm

words.sentence: The ______ pace of recovery frustrated the doctors.
words.example_sentence_hi: वह गर्मी की दोपहर में सुस्त मुद्रा में लेटा था।

words.meaning_en: Deserving praise and commendation
words.example_sentence: Her laudable efforts to educate girls in rural areas won her a national award.

one_word_sub.question: Something that deserves to be praised or commended

words.sentence: The volunteers' ______ work during the disaster relief operation saved many lives.
words.example_sentence_hi: ग्रामीण क्षेत्रों में लड़कियों को शिक्षित करने के उसके प्रशंसनीय प्रयासों ने उसे राष्ट्रीय पुरस्कार दिलाया।

words.meaning_en: Expressed clearly and easy to understand
words.example_sentence: The professor gave a lucid explanation of quantum mechanics.

one_word_sub.question: Something expressed in a clear and easily understood manner

words.meaning_en: Having or showing a wish to do evil to others
words.example_sentence: The malevolent ruler enjoyed watching his subjects suffer.

one_word_sub.question: A person who wishes harm and evil upon others

words.sentence: The ______ conspiracy was designed to destabilise the newly elected government.

words.meaning_en: Showing great attention to detail or being very careful and precise
words.example_sentence: The forensic team conducted a meticulous examination of the crime scene.

one_word_sub.question: A person who is extremely careful and precise in their work

words.sentence: Her ______ preparation for the IAS interview left nothing to chance.
words.example_sentence_hi: फोरेंसिक टीम ने अपराध स्थल की सावधानीपूर्वक जाँच की।

words.meaning_en: Make less severe, serious, or painful
words.example_sentence: Tree plantation drives can help mitigate the effects of climate change.

one_word_sub.question: To lessen the severity or seriousness of something

words.sentence: The new dam was built to ______ the impact of annual floods on the region.

words.meaning_en: Sullen and ill-tempered
words.example_sentence: He sat in morose silence after hearing the bad news.

one_word_sub.question: A person who is gloomy and sullen in temperament

words.sentence: The ______ teenager refused to talk to anyone after the argument.
words.example_sentence_hi: वह बुरी खबर सुनकर उदास चुप्पी में बैठ गया।

words.meaning_en: Annoyingly insensitive or slow to understand
words.example_sentence: His obtuse response to the criticism showed he had missed the point entirely.

one_word_sub.question: A person who is slow to understand or deliberately dense

words.sentence: The ______ bureaucrat failed to understand the urgency of the situation.
words.example_sentence_hi: आलोचना के प्रति उसकी मंद प्रतिक्रिया से पता चला कि वह बात पूरी तरह से चूक गया था।

words.meaning_en: Exclude someone from a society or group
words.example_sentence: The whistleblower was ostracised by his colleagues after exposing the scam.

one_word_sub.question: To exclude someone from a group or social circle

words.sentence: Society tends to ______ those who challenge its deeply held beliefs.
words.example_sentence_hi: घोटाला उजागर करने के बाद व्हिसलब्लोअर को उसके सहयोगियों ने बहिष्कृत कर दिया।

words.meaning_en: A remark or statement that is used too often to be interesting or thoughtful
words.example_sentence: His speech was full of platitudes and offered no real solutions.

one_word_sub.question: A statement that is so common and obvious it has lost its meaning

words.sentence: The politician's address was dismissed as a collection of ______s.
words.example_sentence_hi: उसका भाषण घिसी-पिटी बातों से भरा था और कोई वास्तविक समाधान नहीं दिया।

words.meaning_en: Not securely held; dependent on chance; dangerously uncertain
words.example_sentence: Millions of workers are in precarious employment with no job security.

one_word_sub.question: A situation that is uncertain and potentially dangerous

words.sentence: The mountaineer found herself in a ______ position on the icy cliff.
words.example_sentence_hi: लाखों कामगार बिना नौकरी की सुरक्षा के अनिश्चित रोजगार में हैं।

words.meaning_en: Spending money or resources freely and recklessly; wasteful
words.example_sentence: His prodigal habits depleted his inheritance within two years.

one_word_sub.question: A person who wastefully spends money or resources

words.sentence: The ______ prince spent the entire treasury on personal luxuries.
words.example_sentence_hi: उसकी फिजूलखर्ची की आदतों ने दो साल में उसकी विरासत को समाप्त कर दिया।

words.meaning_en: Not revealing one's thoughts or feelings readily
words.example_sentence: The reticent witness had to be coaxed into giving his account of events.

one_word_sub.question: A person who does not readily share thoughts or feelings

analogy.question: reluctant to speak or reveal feelings : ______

words.sentence: The CEO remained ______ about the company's merger plans during the press conference.
words.example_sentence_hi: संकोची गवाह को अपनी घटना की जानकारी देने के लिए मनाना पड़ा।

words.meaning_en: A person who acts obsequiously towards someone important in order to gain advantage
words.example_sentence: The CEO was surrounded by sycophants who never pointed out his mistakes.

one_word_sub.question: A person who flatters important people to gain personal advantage

analogy.question: one who flatters to gain favour : ______

words.sentence: True advisors give honest feedback; ______s only tell you what you want to hear.
words.example_sentence_hi: सीईओ के आसपास चापलूस थे जो उसकी गलतियाँ कभी नहीं बताते थे।

words.meaning_en: Characterized by conflict, disorder, or confusion; not calm
words.example_sentence: India went through a turbulent phase during the partition of 1947.

one_word_sub.question: A period or situation full of conflict and disorder

words.sentence: The stock market went through a ______ week after the global economic crisis.
words.example_sentence_hi: 1947 के विभाजन के दौरान भारत एक अशांत दौर से गुजरा।

words.meaning_en: Regard with great respect and reverence
words.example_sentence: In Indian culture, elders are venerated as sources of wisdom.

one_word_sub.question: To regard someone or something with deep respect and reverence

words.sentence: The memorial was built to ______ those who gave their lives for the nation.
words.example_sentence_hi: भारतीय संस्कृति में बुजुर्गों को ज्ञान के स्रोत के रूप में श्रद्धा दी जाती है।
